title: iris spuria subsp. subbarbata
intro: Iris spuria subsp. subbarbata, also known as the Spuria Iris or False Iris, is a type of flowering plant that belongs to the iris family and is native to Europe and Western Asia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, which can be used to treat various health issues. The health benefits of Iris spuria subsp. subbarbata include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory problems, and treat skin conditions such as eczema and acne. Herbal preparations of the plant include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be made by infusing the dried flowers and roots in hot water or a solvent like ethanol.
